denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

Cigna has completed prior authorization review of the request submitted by your provider for continued Phase II supervised outpatient cardiac rehabilitation for heart failure with reduced ejection fraction. After review, the request is denied.

Your plan covers a limited number of outpatient cardiac rehabilitation sessions per plan year. Our records indicate that the annual visit limit for outpatient cardiac rehabilitation services has been exceeded for the current plan year. Under your plan's stated benefit limits and the applicable Cigna Coverage Policy, additional sessions are not a covered benefit for the remainder of this plan year, so the requested continued cardiac rehabilitation does not meet the plan's coverage criteria at this time.

A peer-to-peer review window was provided verbally by telephone to your provider's office and is not confirmed in this letter.

If you disagree with this determination, you may request an internal appeal as described in your plan materials and submit additional clinical information for review. Your treating provider may also contact Cigna to discuss the visit-limit determination for the requested cardiac rehabilitation sessions.

This determination addresses coverage under the terms of your plan only and is not medical advice or a statement about the care your treating providers may recommend.

Sincerely,
Cigna Medical Coverage Review
clinical_context: The patient is a 64-year-old man with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, with a left ventricular ejection fraction of 30 percent and NYHA class II to III symptoms on optimized guideline-directed medical therapy. During the cardiac rehabilitation sessions completed so far he has shown objective functional gains, including an improved six-minute walk distance, a higher peak oxygen uptake, and a reduction in NYHA class. Cardiac rehabilitation is a Class I guideline-recommended therapy for heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, and the additional sessions are medically necessary to consolidate these gains, so the blanket visit-cap denial ignores documented benefit and active medical necessity. The offered peer-to-peer review window was communicated only verbally by telephone to office staff and was never confirmed in writing, so the treating cardiologist cannot verify or dispute the deadline. The determination also omits the ERISA-required disclosures of the specific reasons for the adverse benefit determination, the plan provisions relied upon, and the member's right to bring a civil action under 29 U.S.C. section 1133, rendering the notice procedurally defective.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches the assigned cell field-for-field (Cigna, Prior Authorization, cardiology, 56-70, M, visit_limit_exceeded); dx HFrEF (I50.20) is cardiology; continued Phase II cardiac rehabilitation is realistic; denial_rationale_seed leads with visit_limit_exceeded (annual cardiac rehab visit cap exceeded); null employer_archetype acceptable for age 64.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Cardiac rehab visit caps are real (commonly 36 Phase II sessions); a 'limit exceeded, not a covered benefit this plan year' denial mirrors genuine insurer logic; cardiac rehab is a Class I therapy for HFrEF; the benefit-cap denial ignoring documented functional gains is realistically exploitable, and the verbal-only P2P window and omitted ERISA disclosures are real-world defects on a self-funded ERISA plan; 64M fits HFrEF.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Branded Cigna throughout with a 'Cigna Coverage Policy' reference and 'Cigna Medical Coverage Review' sign-off, but lacks Cigna's distinctive clinical tells (no Cigna Medical Necessity Guidelines / MCG, no step-therapy framing); as a benefit-exhaustion visit-limit denial it reads generic-but-not-wrong (no wrong-insurer tells), keeping it at 3.
insurer_voice.improvement: Add Cigna's signature clinical tells (Cigna Medical Necessity Guidelines / MCG).

denial_logic.reasoning: Cites 'your plan's stated benefit limits and the applicable Cigna Coverage Policy' (plausible) without stating the numeric cap or sessions used, and folds the limit into a circular coverage conclusion; appropriately hand-wavy and exploitable for the visit_limit_exceeded tactic; no hallucinated guideline.

clinical_realism.reasoning: HFrEF with LVEF 30% and NYHA II-III is internally consistent; Phase II cardiac rehab is a genuine Class I HFrEF therapy; the cited outcome measures (improved six-minute walk distance, higher peak VO2, reduced NYHA class) are the validated metrics; coherent trajectory; no invented or biology-violating content.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Phase II supervised cardiac rehabilitation is a Class I guideline-recommended (ACC/AHA/HFSA; CMS-covered per HF-ACTION) intervention for stable chronic HFrEF; the visit-cap framing is a coverage dispute, not a clinical mismatch; clean standard-of-care match.

diversity_delta.reasoning: The cardiac-rehab visit_limit neighbour (Aetna, post-STEMI/PCI CAD) shares the verbatim treatment string 'Continued Phase II supervised outpatient cardiac rehabilitation (additional sessions beyond the annual visit cap)' and the same denial reason; only payer (Cigna vs Aetna) and dx (HFrEF vs post-STEMI) differ, and the denial trajectory reads essentially the same - a same-trajectory collision warranting a 3.
diversity_delta.improvement: Reword the tx string and/or switch the denial axis so it does not collide with the Aetna cardiac-rehab visit_limit case.

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.228 (nearest=case_175_cigna_priorauth), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: Adult 64M HFrEF cardiac-rehab visit-cap dispute; framing is curative/restorative (functional gains), no hospice/DNR/EOL; no SI/self-harm/abuse/IPV, not minor-gender care; Cigna self-funded commercial plan with no Medicare/Medicaid framing.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Patient (64M), dx (HFrEF), and requested continued Phase II cardiac rehab agree across letter and context; the verbal-only P2P detail matches between documents; the benefit-cap vs medical-necessity tension is appealable disagreement, not a contradiction; coherent timeline; no laterality/drug/date conflicts.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Formal bureaucratic insurer boilerplate and a dense comma-spliced clinical recitation with a single parallel list (explicitly not a tell); no 'As an AI' tics, GPT vocabulary, listicles, or marketing cadence; nothing at the >=0.7 conspicuous-tell bar.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold bureaucratic Cigna register ('the request is denied', 'does not meet the plan's coverage criteria at this time', verbal-only P2P) with no apology or marketing; clinical context is plain analytic prose with only mild legal framing, not emotional pleading.

financial_auditor.reasoning: No mention of member out-of-pocket liability, dollar amounts, or coinsurance; denies on a benefit-limit basis with appropriate commercial framing; no Medicare/Medicaid conflation. Silence on cost liability is standard practice.

legal_auditor.reasoning: Mentions internal appeal vaguely and strategically omits ERISA disclosures and external review rights (a realistic attack vector); the single statutory reference (29 U.S.C. 1133) lives in the clinical context and is accurate for an ERISA plan; no hallucinated statutes; no Medicare framing on this commercial plan.

demographic_validator.reasoning: 64M with HFrEF is a strong epidemiologic match (HFrEF prevalence rises with age, male-predominant); consistent male pronouns; gender-neutral letter; treatment age/dx-appropriate; no stereotyping.

scope_guard.reasoning: Cigna in-scope, Prior Authorization, self_funded ERISA commercial (employer-sponsored, not a public program), adult 64, member-facing internal appeal only, no live-filing/attorney/court-representation language; the 29 U.S.C. 1133 reference describes the omitted disclosure, not litigation; no Medicare/Medicaid/marketplace framing.

date_sanity.reasoning: No explicit calendar dates, denial/request dates, or numeric appeal window; the P2P window is explicitly not confirmed in writing and the clinical timeline is relative ('completed so far'); no contradiction (so not a 1), but no affirmative 2025-2026 dated stack either - the middle anchor.
date_sanity.improvement: Add explicit 2026 service-request/denial dates, an appeal window, and date-anchored rehab-session timeline.

citation_traceability.reasoning: References only generic umbrella terms ('the applicable Cigna Coverage Policy', 'your plan's stated benefit limits', 'your plan materials') with no module/edition/bulletin code/page and no fabricated alphanumeric identifier; the realistic frustrating vagueness the rubric rewards.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Strong clinical case (Class I HFrEF indication with documented objective gains - improved 6MWD, higher peak VO2, reduced NYHA class - that the blanket cap ignores) plus two independent procedural defects: a verbal-only, unwritten peer-to-peer window and omitted ERISA disclosures (specific reasons, plan provisions, right to civil action under 29 U.S.C. 1133). Easy appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: ERISA-required disclosures omitted (specific reasons, plan provisions relied upon, right to civil action under 29 U.S.C. 1133), rendering the notice procedurally defective
Peer-to-peer window communicated only verbally and never confirmed in writing, so the cardiologist cannot verify or dispute the deadline
Blanket visit-cap rationale ignores Class I guideline-recommended therapy and documented objective functional gains (6MWD, peak VO2, NYHA class reduction)
strong_defenses: The denial invokes a concrete non-clinical basis - a stated annual plan benefit/visit limit and the applicable Cigna Coverage Policy - and provides procedural scaffolding (a named internal appeal pathway, the option to submit additional clinical information, and a provider route to discuss the visit-limit determination)
